location.office_time_required: Flex First — no mandated office time; regular in-person events

level_signals: 5+ years building and shipping consumer-facing commerce products
2+ years building LLM-powered products in production
Base pay CA/NY range $250K-$264K, consistent with Principal IC
Represents product to senior leadership and external retail partners

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience building and shipping consumer-facing commerce products end-to-end, with 2+ years building LLM-powered products in production
must_haves.qualifier: consumer-facing commerce end-to-end; including 2+ years LLM-powered products in production

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven track record launching at least one 0-to-1 product to general availability with measurable impact on adoption, conversion, revenue, or GTV
Technical fluency with AI/ML systems and data flows; ability to partner with engineering and machine learning on dependencies and scope tradeoffs
Experience defining product OKRs, with significant experience using experimentation to improve products and achieve desired outcomes
Exceptional communication and presentation skills with senior leadership and external enterprise partners
Bachelor's degree in a technical, quantitative, or related field, or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience across both consumer products and B2B/enterprise offerings
Background spanning startup and large company environments
Domain expertise in ecommerce, retail, grocery, or marketplace dynamics
Experience building or improving LLM agent systems and products
Familiarity with experimentation at scale (A/B testing) and with trust, safety, privacy, and compliance considerations for GenAI products.

scope.surface: Cart Assistant — enterprise-grade, consumer shopping agent purpose-built for grocery; agentic commerce experiences across Instacart surfaces
scope.business_model: B2C consumer shopping + B2B enterprise retailer-facing platform
core_responsibilities: Set and own the roadmap, translating consumer and retailer needs into a clear vision, milestones, and launch plans
Lead product discovery, uncover customer pain points and market opportunities, and partner with engineering and design to deliver solutions
Drive measurable results including product adoption and engagement outcomes. Establish and maintain metrics, dashboards, and regular business reviews
Lead cross-functional execution with Engineering/ML/DS/Design to execute the roadmap, run rapid experiments, ship iterative improvements, and pivot based on learnings
Partner with ML, model design, and content design to improve core agent performance and behavior using state-of-the-art methods
Represent the product to senior leadership and external retail partners
Work with business development, market strategy, and GTM teams to align product and GTM plans
Scale Cart Assistant across Instacart surfaces and products, driving top line business impact

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation ranges fully disclosed across US geographies — highly competitive ($209K–$264K base depending on location)
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Fully remote eligible (#LI-Remote, Flex First policy)
Clear 0->1 product scope with named product (Cart Assistant) — not vague
Explicit acknowledgement of intersection of generative AI, consumer, and B2B — well-defined domain
High-visibility, fast-paced role with senior leadership and external partner exposure
red_flags: Hard requirement of 2+ years LLM-powered products in production is specific and may eliminate many otherwise-qualified PMs given how recently LLMs became production-ready
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Very high compensation floor (Principal level) signals a demanding bar with limited flexibility on experience requirements
Role spans both consumer UX and enterprise B2B; candidates with only one side of that experience may be disadvantaged

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp ranges + 8+ years + Principal title)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — B2C consumer + B2B retailer platform)
compensation.base_range_usd min/max (derived from lowest 'all other states' floor and highest CA/NY ceiling across the tiered table)
location.visa_sponsorship (not stated in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The JD lists four distinct pay tiers by US state geography; base_range_usd is reported as the full span ($209K–$264K) but actual offer depends on candidate's permanent work location.

Flex First policy described as employees choosing where they work best with 'regular in-person events' — no specific office time percentage stated, making it closer to remote-us than hybrid, but some in-person cadence is expected.
